matches.moment: With Onana absent, Belgium's midfield leaves a central gap that Spain exploits on a turnover to open the scoring; Lukaku's late introduction produces Belgium's reply, extending his pattern of scoring in three straight sub appearances, but too late to force extra time.

verdict: Spain arrive at SoFi Stadium as the tournament's most statistically complete side - five competitive matches, zero goals conceded, the longest World Cup clean-sheet run on record - but their attacking output has been shaped by opponent quality as much as their own class, dropping to a single stoppage-time-level goal the one time they faced a side of genuine standard in Portugal. Belgium's story is the inverse: a group stage built on defensive solidity has given way to two knockout wins, over Senegal and the United States, that flatter their underlying numbers badly, with 3.18 and 2.06 expected goals conceded in those matches respectively against only three goals actually let in - a gap Thibaut Courtois and finishing variance have covered, not tactical structure. Losing Amadou Onana for the rest of the tournament removes the one player built to close that gap, at the exact moment Belgium face the side with the division's best central midfield pairing in Pedri and Rodri. The data does not support a rout - Belgium retain individual match-winners in De Bruyne, Trossard and a fresh Lukaku, and Spain's own attacking sample has never been tested by a side this good - but it points to Spain's control translating into a one-goal margin rather than a repeat clean sheet, with Belgium's structural defensive leakage, not their scoreline, deciding how this one goes.

players.name: Mikel Oyarzabal
players.stat: 4 goals, 1 assist in 4 matches - Spain's outright top scorer
players.watch: First real test of the tournament's poacher instincts against a knockout-tested Belgium backline missing Onana's cover

players.name: Kevin De Bruyne
players.stat: Restored to the XI after being rested vs USA; shifting into a more central role to cover Onana's absence
players.watch: Whether he can dictate tempo without Onana's protection, against Spain's Pedri-Rodri pivot

players.name: Romelu Lukaku
players.stat: 3 goals - all as a substitute across 3 consecutive appearances - more tournament goals than any other Belgium player
players.watch: Whether Garcia turns to him from the start given the magnitude of the tie
